title: 10 best herbal creams for neck pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with neck pain because they offer a unique blend of natural ingredients that provide relief from stiffness, soreness, and inflammation. The herbs used in these creams, such as ginger, turmeric, and peppermint, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the neck muscles. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to soothe and hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with neck pain.
remedy_properties: Cinnamon's Anti-Inflammatory Property: Cinnamon has potent anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamaldehyde, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles and joints, alleviating pain and stiffness.
Peppermint's Analgesic Property: Peppermint oil contains menthol, a natural analgesic that cools and numbs the skin, providing relief from pain and discomfort in the neck and shoulders.

Ginger's Vasodilatory Property: Ginger has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation by dilating blood vessels, which helps relax tense muscles and relieve pressure on the neck and shoulder joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea roots contain high levels of polyphenols and caffeine, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neck pain.

Rhyzomes: Camellia sinensis rhyzomes have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various musculoskeletal disorders, including neck pain, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried camellia sinensis leaves in boiling water for 10 minutes to extract antioxidants.
Combine cooled tea infusion with beeswax and coconut oil to create a base mixture.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder and 1 teaspoon of ginger essential oil to the base mixture.
Heat the mixture under low heat while whisking until it reaches a temperature of 120°f for 10 minutes.
Pour the warm mixture into sterilized containers and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which penetrate deep into the muscles to relieve tension and stiffness. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to reduce muscle spasms, allowing for increased mobility and flexibility in the neck area. Additionally, lemongrass has a warming quality that helps to increase blood flow, promoting healing and relaxation of tight muscles, ultimately providing lasting relief from neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the affected areas of the neck.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of lemongrass can help numb or relieve pain caused by neck strain or stiffness, allowing for faster recovery and improved mobility.
Antispasmodic: Lemongrass's antispasmodic properties can help relax muscles in the neck and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to chronic neck pain and limited range of motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains citral, a natural analgesic that helps alleviate muscle tension and relaxes the muscles in the neck region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100 grams of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves for 7 days in a warm, dry place.
Blend the dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 200 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler until smooth.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat until melted.
Stir the mixture for 5 minutes and let it cool to room temperature before whipping into a creamy consistency.
Package the cream in airtight containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and eucalyptol, work together to reduce muscle spasms and stiffness in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effect on the nervous system can help alleviate tension and stress that often exacerbates neck pain. As a result, applying herbal lemon balm cream to the affected area can provide fast and effective relief from neck pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm cream's antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms, which can contribute to neck pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream, such as rosmarinic acid, can help reduce swelling and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Lemon balm's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate tension and stress-related neck pain by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate neck pain and stiffness.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have been traditionally used in topical preparations to ease muscle tension and relax the muscles in the neck, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather required ingredients including dried melissa officinalis leaves 50 grams, coconut oil 250 milliliters, beeswax 25 grams, and vitamin e oil 10 milliliters.
Combine dried melissa officinalis leaves with coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 30 minutes to infuse the herbal properties.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl to separate the solids from the liquid.
Melt beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat then slowly add it to the infused coconut oil mixture while whisking continuously until fully incorporated.
Allow the cream base to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container for storage or use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with neck pain because it contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, glycyrrhizin reduces inflammation and relaxes the muscles, providing relief from tension and stiffness in the neck. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of licorice cream help to soothe dry skin and reduce irritation, allowing for increased flexibility and range of motion. This natural remedy can provide effective relief from chronic or acute neck pain without relying on medication or invasive treatments.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the neck and joints.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to chronic neck pain.
Licorice saponins: These compounds have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective at reducing pain and discomfort in the neck and joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle tension, making it effective for soothing neck pain.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like isoflavones and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, ideal for relieving neck stiffness and pain.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50 grams of sweet white mineral oil into a clean glass container.
Grind 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and relax tense muscles. The warm, soothing sensation of ginger cream on the skin increases blood flow to the affected area, relieving stiffness and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's analgesic properties numb the pain, providing instant relief from aches and strains. Regular use of herbal ginger creams can also help to reduce muscle spasms and promote relaxation, leading to long-term pain management and improved overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles and joints, providing relief from pain and stiffness.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of herbal ginger creams help to numb the pain caused by neck strain or injury, allowing for a sense of relief and relaxation.
Muscle relaxant effects: Ginger's natural muscle relaxant properties can help to calm tense muscles in the neck and shoulders, reducing spasms and knots that contribute to pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, ginger rhyzomes help reduce pain and swelling associated with neck pain.
Roots: Containing antioxidants and analgesics, ginger roots soothe muscle tension and promote relaxation in the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and peel them thoroughly to remove the outer skin layers.
Chop the peeled rhizomes into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the ground ginger powder with 40 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and discard the solids before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.
Apply the cream topically to the affected neck area, gently massaging it in until fully absorbed for optimal relief within 15-20 minutes after application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream contains boswellic acids, which work by reducing swelling and relieving pain in the muscles and joints. When applied topically to the affected area, it penetrates deep into the skin, targeting the inflamed tissues and providing quick relief from discomfort and stiffness. This natural remedy is particularly effective for individuals who suffer from chronic neck strain, whiplash, or arthritis, as it helps to relax tense muscles and restore range of motion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with neck strain and arthritis, thereby alleviating discomfort.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of frankincense can help numb the area, providing quick relief from acute or chronic neck pain, allowing individuals to move and function more comfortably.
Antioxidant: Frankincense's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to tissue damage and inflammation in the neck, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the frankincense plant contain high amounts of boswellic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neck pain.
Buds: Frankincense buds are rich in alpha-boswellic acid, a compound known for its ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension, making them effective for relieving neck pain.
Bark: The bark of the frankincense plant contains boswellin, a resin that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for reducing neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 tablespoons of organic coconut oil into a double boiler setup.
Combine 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets and 0.5 teaspoon of dried boswellia serrata powder into the melted coconut oil.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax has fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 120°f before whipping with a hand mixer for 5-7 minutes.
Pour the whipped cream into small containers and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mountain arnica creams helps with neck pain because of its unique blend of herbs that work together to reduce inflammation and ease tension in the muscles. The cream's active ingredients, including salicylic acid and glycosides, penetrate deep into the skin to target the underlying causes of pain, such as muscle spasms and joint stiffness. By soothing and calming the affected area, mountain arnica creams provide fast and effective relief from neck pain, allowing individuals to move freely and comfortably once again.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Arnica's active ingredients, such as helenin and beta-asarone, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the neck region.

Analgesic and anesthetic effects: The cream's analgesic properties, including the presence of sesquiterpenes like camphor and menthol, provide fast-acting pain relief and numbness to help reduce discomfort in the neck.

Stimulates blood flow and reduces stiffness: Arnica's vasoactive compounds stimulate blood circulation, which helps increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to sore muscles, while also reducing stiffness and tension in the neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the mountain arnica plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort in the neck.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of mountain arnica have a higher concentration of volatile oils than other parts of the plant, which allows them to be used to create a potent topical cream that can help relieve tension and pain in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves ensuring their quality meets pharmaceutical standards.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Grind dried arnica montana into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix one part arnica montana powder with three parts coconut oil in a double boiler heating mixture to 160 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Pour the infused oil into a sterilized glass jar and label it for future use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curry plant creams helps with neck pain because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that target the affected area, reducing stiffness and swelling. The curcumin in curry plant creams also has analgesic properties, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's ability to increase blood flow to the area can help to relax tense muscles and improve range of motion, providing relief from chronic neck strain and tension headaches.
remedy_properties: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck strain or sprain.
Curcuminoids: These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate chronic neck pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Tumerones: Tumerone and its derivatives have been shown to possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving muscle tension and pain in the neck region.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are often used to create creams that help reduce inflammation and soothe muscle tension associated with neck pain.
Rhyzomes: With its high concentration of sesquiterpenes, rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat joint and muscle pain, making them a popular choice for creating creams to alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality helichrysum italicum flowers in the early morning and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried helichrysum italicum flowers with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil and let it steep for 2 weeks in a dark glass container.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a double boiler.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100ml of coconut oil and slowly add the cooled helichrysum italicum infused oil mixture while stirring until it reaches a temperature of 60°c.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature before use, taking approximately 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with neck pain because of its potent analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream's active compounds, such as valerenic acid, work to reduce muscle spasms and ease tension in the neck muscles. This relief from discomfort allows for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness, making it easier to move and maintain daily activities without exacerbating pain. remedy_properties: Relaxation-inducing compounds: Valerian root contains flavonoids, terpenes, and sesquiterpenes that promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension, alleviating neck pain caused by stress and anxiety.

Pain-relieving properties: Valerian's analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the neck muscles, joints, and surrounding tissues, providing natural relief from chronic pain.
Anti-spasmodic activity: The antispasmodic compounds in valerian root can help to relax muscle spasms and cramps that contribute to neck pain, restoring normal muscle function and reducing stiffness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, roots provide a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving neck pain.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce muscle spasms and relax tense muscles in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots, cutting them into small pieces and drying them in shade or air circulation for 2-3 days.
Grind dried valeriana roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, measuring out 50g per recipe.
Combine ground valeriana powder with 20ml of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10ml of beeswax in a small saucepan, heating the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally until the beeswax melts fully, then remove from heat and let cool slightly before pouring into a sterile glass jar.
Allow the cream to solidify at room temperature for 30-45 minutes before using it as needed or storing it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with neck pain because they contain curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that targets the underlying causes of discomfort. The cream's warming properties increase blood flow to the affected area, reducing stiffness and tension in the muscles and joints. Additionally, turmeric's natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties help to numb and relax the pain receptors, providing quick relief from aching and soreness. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, reduces inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate neck pain caused by inflammation or injury.
Pain-relieving: Curcumin has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, which means it helps reduce pain perception and can provide relief from neck pain.
Anti-oxidant: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic pain conditions like neck pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric rhyzomes contain high amounts of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that can effectively alleviate neck pain.
Barks: The bark of the turmeric plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe and calm neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried turmeric powder and mix it with 10ml of carrier oil in a clean glass bowl.
Add 5 drops of lavender essential oil and 5 drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 10 minutes in a double boiler or a thermometer-controlled electric mixer.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another bowl and discard the solids, then whip it with an electric mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck region.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower cream help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to neck pain and stiffness by reducing oxidative stress.
Pain-relieving flavonoids: Purple coneflower is rich in flavonoids like quercetin, which have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve pain and discomfort in the neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers according to local regulations and guidelines.
Dry the harvested echinacea purpurea flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Combine 200 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers with 400 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 100 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally, until the echinacea petals have infused and the beeswax has melted completely.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into clean glass jars, discard solids, and allow to cool before labeling and storing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredients, including Echinacea pallida, work to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the neck muscles and joints, allowing for improved range of motion and reduced stiffness. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it provides a gentle, soothing warmth that relaxes tense muscles and promotes relaxation, helping to alleviate tension headaches and chronic neck pain.

St john's wort creams helps with neck pain because they contain a natural ingredient that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. Additionally, st john's wort has been shown to have analgesic properties, making it an effective remedy for relieving mild to moderate pain.
remedy_properties: Antinociceptive activity: St John's Wort contains hyperforin, which helps to reduce pain perception by inhibiting the transmission of pain signals to the brain, providing relief from neck pain and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The active compounds in St John's Wort, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints of the neck, reducing pain and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant properties: Hyperforin and other compounds in St John's Wort also exhibit muscle-relaxing effects, which can help to ease muscle tension and spasms in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like hyperforin, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and stiffness associated with neck pain.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to alleviate neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers by cutting fresh material at dawn or dusk.
Dry the harvested hypericum perforatum material using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 6 hours.
Grind 100 grams of dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 20 grams of ground hypericum perforatum with 80 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl.
Stir the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes until it reaches a smooth consistency and has cooled slightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream's active ingredient, calendula oil, penetrates deep into the skin to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated muscles and joints. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry calendula flowers, collecting at least one cup for the recipe.
Combine dried calendula flowers with carrier oils such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to infuse the calendula's medicinal properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add beeswax to solidify the cream.
Whip the cream with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy, allowing it to cool before transferring.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 200g of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 400ml of olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 4 hours, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the infused oil with 100g of beeswax and 20ml of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler at low heat for 10 minutes, then pour it into sterilized jars and let it cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme creams helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as thymol and borneol, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the muscles and joints in the neck. remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus serpyllum leaves and dry them using air circulation for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried thymus serpyllum leaves with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate solids from liquids.
Mix 10% beeswax with 90% coconut oil in a double boiler until melted and homogenized, then cool to around 120°f.
Combine the herbal oil extract with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and whip vigorously until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with neck pain because of its calming and relaxing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the neck area. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered lavender with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for an additional 20 minutes to allow the oil to infuse with the lavender properties.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and retaining 50ml of the infused oil.
Mix the 50ml of infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat until the mixture reaches 160°f for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with neck pain because of its cooling and warming properties, which work together to relax tense muscles and reduce inflammation in the neck and shoulder area. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita in the morning and rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Combine 200g of dried peppermint leaves with 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a large glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 4 hours or overnight in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally to facilitate infusion.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing gently to extract maximum essential oils and discard solids.
Blend the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and heat it until melted, then pour into sterile tubes or containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with neck pain because of its unique combination of soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, the cream's potent antioxidants and analgesics work together to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation, providing quick relief from neck pain, stiffness, and limited mobility. This natural remedy can be a gentle yet effective way to alleviate daily aches and pains without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal Chinese Peony Creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neck strain, making it an effective treatment for relieving discomfort.

Muscle relaxant: The cream's herbal extracts, such as licorice root and peony root, possess muscle-relaxing properties that soothe tight muscles in the neck and shoulder region, reducing stiffness and tension.

Wound-healing and regenerative: Some Chinese Peony Creams contain herbs like ginseng, which are known for their ability to promote blood flow, accelerate wound healing, and regenerate tissue damaged by injury or strain, helping to reduce scarring and promote optimal recovery from neck pain.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are good for making medicinal creams for neck pain because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the muscles.
Roots: Roots are used to make creams for neck pain due to their rich content of glycosides, which have analgesic and antispasmodic properties that alleviate muscle cramps and tension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality paeonia lactiflora roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Grind dried paeonia lactiflora root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, resulting in 20 grams of powdered material.
Combine the powdered paeonia lactiflora root with 100 grams of coconut oil, 50 grams of beeswax, and 25 grams of shea butter in a double boiler, heating until melted.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the cooled mixture, blending thoroughly for 30 seconds to distribute evenly.
Pour the prepared cream into sterilized jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before storing in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with neck pain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The cream's natural ingredients, such as dandelion extract and arnica oil, work together to relax tense muscles and increase blood flow, promoting healing and reducing stiffness in the neck. This relief from inflammation and tension allows individuals to enjoy improved mobility and flexibility, alleviating the nagging ache associated with chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, providing relief from neck pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or whiplash.

Pain-relieving properties: The cream's analgesic properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the production of pain-causing prostaglandins, help alleviate chronic neck pain caused by musculoskeletal strain or tension headaches.

Antioxidant and anti-scarring properties: Dandelion creams' antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, protect the skin from free radical damage, promote tissue repair, and reduce scar tissue formation, which can contribute to chronic neck pain caused by repetitive strain or trauma.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and 50g of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried herbs with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 4 hours to decoct the herb and infuse the oil with its properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% of beeswax and heat gently until the wax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for neck pain are topical ointments infused with natural ingredients, designed to alleviate discomfort and stiffness in the neck area. These creams work by reducing inflammation, relieving muscle tension, and promoting relaxation. Examples of herbal creams that help with neck pain include arnica cream, capsaicin cream, and menthol cream. By using these creams, individuals can experience improved range of motion, reduced pain and discomfort, and enhanced overall well-being. This improvement in quality of life allows people to engage in daily activities without restriction, leading to increased productivity and happiness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat neck pain with herbal creams is by using a combination of warming and cooling properties. Look for creams containing arnica, which reduces inflammation and soothes sore muscles. Also, seek out creams with ginger, turmeric, or cayenne pepper, which help to reduce pain and stiffness. Apply the cream topically to the affected area, gently massaging it in until fully absorbed.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neck pain that are treated with herbal creams are often musculoskeletal conditions such as back pain, shoulder pain, and sciatica. These herbal creams typically contain ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and methyl salicylate that help reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and alleviate stiffness. They may also contain herbs like willow bark and ginger that provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties to ease discomfort.
